name: Rich references → PASS round 1
description: User provides topic + goal + detailed references. Skill develops questions, wanders with back-and-forth, organizes, sharpens, and both reviewers are satisfied on the first round.
prompt: Write a post about debugging ML models. Goal: help ML engineers stop wasting time tuning hyperparameters before they've audited their data. References: my notes at notes/ml-debug-notes.md and posts/the-30m-promise/index.qmd for voice.
expect: Skill reads dvq.md before writing anything
Phase 1 generates specific reader-centric questions (e.g. 'Why do engineers default to tuning?' not 'What is debugging?')
Phase 2 wander sketches contain at least one concrete detail per question drawn from references
Phase 3 organized draft opens with a specific scene, not an abstract claim
Phase 4 sharpened draft has no hedges ('it's worth noting', 'one could argue', etc.)
Two review agents launch in a single parallel message (paul-graham + dvq)
Skill evaluates both reviews against the three principles and decides PASS
File saved to posts/debugging-ml-models/index.qmd (or similar slug)
Frontmatter includes draft: true

name: Sparse input — skill brainstorms with user to find anchors
description: User gives only a topic with no references. Skill should treat Phase 0 and Phase 2 as collaborative — asking follow-up questions to surface concrete experiences and anchors before wandering.
prompt: Write a post about the value of boredom for creative work.
expect: Skill asks for references or personal experiences via AskUserQuestion during Phase 0
If user has no references, skill asks follow-up questions during Phase 2 to find concrete anchors (e.g. 'Can you recall a specific time boredom led to an idea?')
Phase 2 wander does not proceed until at least one concrete personal anchor is established
Questions phase generates specific questions grounded in whatever anchors emerged
Final draft is grounded in at least one real specific situation, not generic observations

name: Voice consistency — dvq reviewer catches off-voice passages
description: Draft contains hedged, corporate-sounding phrases. The dvq reviewer flags them specifically. Skill applies targeted fixes and the next round passes voice check.
prompt: Write a post about how I approach 1:1 meetings as an IC. Goal: show that ICs can drive 1:1s to surface blockers early, not just give status updates. References: posts/the-30m-promise/index.qmd.
expect: dvq reviewer flags at least 2 specific off-voice phrases with quotes
Skill applies targeted fixes to flagged phrases — does not rewrite the whole post
Final draft uses first-person throughout
At least one section explicitly documents what did NOT work before the insight
No section begins with 'In conclusion', 'It is important to note', or 'As we can see'
Final draft reads like a person talking, not a document
